tutorial.step_welcome_content: This quick tour covers the key features so you can start grading smarter. You can skip at any time and restart from Settings whenever you like.
tutorial.step_rubrics_content: Create rubrics with custom criteria, performance levels, and point scales. Link criteria to CEFR language standards, add a vocabulary list for automatic document analysis, or insert pre-built speaking dimensions. Version history lets you restore any previous rubric state. You can also click 'Compare' next to a rubric to grade two students side by side.
tutorial.step_students_content: Add students and organise them into classes. Each student gets a profile showing their grades, progress over time, and CEFR achievement level. You can also configure VO tracks per class.
tutorial.step_grading_content: Open a rubric from the Rubrics page and pick a student to grade. Score each criterion, attach evidence files, record voice feedback, or use the Comment Bank for reusable snippets. Hit 'Save & Next' to move through your class efficiently.
tutorial.step_comparative_content: On the Rubrics page, click 'Compare' to grade two students side by side. The app adaptively suggests adjustments based on their relative performance — ideal for calibration sessions and anchor-based grading.
tutorial.step_cefr_content: Track language proficiency across your entire school using the Common European Framework of Reference. See which CEFR levels each student has reached per skill, spot class-wide trends, and monitor progress over time.
tutorial.step_attachments_content: Upload PDFs, Word documents, or images as reference materials or student submissions. During grading, click 'Analyse' to automatically extract text via OCR and detect vocabulary words and grammar structures — powered by Tesseract and LanguageTool.
tutorial.step_statistics_content: View grade distributions, per-criterion performance, CEFR achievement rates, and learning goal progress across your class. Use these insights to spot trends and identify students who need extra support.
tutorial.step_comments_content: Build a reusable library of feedback snippets organised by tag. During grading, insert them with one click to save time and keep your feedback consistent.
tutorial.step_export_content: Export graded rubrics as formatted Word documents (using custom templates), PDFs, or CSV files. Export a single student or your entire class in one go.
tutorial.step_settings_content: Change the theme and accent colour, manage grade scales, set your language (English or Dutch), and configure the Standards API key. You can restart this tour at any time from the Settings page.

settings.title: Settings
settings.language_help: Choose your preferred language for the interface.
settings.admin_dashboard_desc: Manage users, schools, and student data retention.
settings.cambridge_api_help: Optionally connect to the Cambridge Dictionary API for richer CEFR word-level data. When a key is provided, the vocabulary profiler uses Cambridge as a secondary lookup to fill gaps in the bundled CEFR-J dataset.
settings.cambridge_api_placeholder: Paste your Cambridge Dictionary API key here...
settings.cambridge_api_register: Register for a free key at
settings.standards_help_1: To link standards (CCSS, NGSS, etc.) from the Common Standards Project, you need a free API key.
settings.standards_setup_2: Copy your API Key and paste it above.
settings.standards_setup_3: Important: Add this app's URL ({{origin}}) to the Allowed Origins list on their dashboard, or requests will be blocked by CORS.
settings.comment_bank_help: {{count}} saved comments. Manage your reusable feedback snippets.
settings.export_templates_help: Upload blank .docx rubrics — their column headers and colours will be used when exporting to Word.
settings.empty_state_templates: No templates uploaded yet. Click Upload Template to add one.
settings.backup_help: Export saves all rubrics, students, grades, and settings to a JSON file. Import restores everything.
settings.backup_preview_subtitle: Review the changes below. Importing will replace all current data with the backup contents.
settings.backup_preview_warning: This action cannot be undone. Export your current data first if you need a safety copy.
settings.delete_scale_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this grade scale? This cannot be undone.
settings.cloud_sync_help: Connect your Microsoft account to sync rubrics across devices via OneDrive or SharePoint.
settings.ms_client_id_help: Optional: Use your own Azure AD Client ID for custom integration.
settings.comparisons_limit_help: Maximum comparisons each student can appear in. The session stops when all students have reached this limit. 0 means no limit.
settings.overdue_threshold_help: Students not graded in this many days appear in the notification bell.
settings.sharing_no_users: No other users found. Make sure colleagues have signed in at least once.
settings.notify_on_grade_help: Sends an email to the student when you save a grade. Requires SMTP to be configured in your Supabase project.

rubricList.title: Rubrics
rubricList.create_first_instruction: Create your first rubric to get started.
rubricList.delete_rubric_warning: This will permanently delete the rubric and all associated student scores. This cannot be undone.
rubricList.essay_pick_class_desc: Select a class to assign the "{{rubric}}" essay to. Each student will get their own unique link.
rubricList.share_modal_desc: Share "{{rubric}}" with another teacher. They must have an account in this RubricMaker instance.
rubricList.share_notfound: No account found for {{email}}. Ask them to sign in to RubricMaker first.

tests.create_first_instruction: Create your first test to start assessing students.
tests.delete_test_warning: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
tests.description_placeholder: Notes for yourself about this test…
tests.shuffle_questions_label: Shuffle question order for each student
tests.require_seb_help: Students can only open this test inside Safe Exam Browser, which locks down their device during the test.
tests.no_questions_instruction: Add your first question to get started.
tests.expected_answer_help: Used for exact-match auto-scoring. Leave empty to grade this question manually.
tests.standards_api_key_required: Add a Common Standards Project API key in Settings → Standards Integration to link academic standards.

results.title: Test results
results.not_found: This submission could not be found.
results.integrity_no_events: No proctoring events were recorded during this session.
results.adjuster_description: Apply a uniform point adjustment to every student's score to move the class average toward a target percentage. This is reversible at any time.
results.adjuster_no_submissions: No submissions yet for this test.
results.adjustment_audited_note: An adjustment is currently applied. Reverting restores each student's raw score.
results.import_description: Paste the submission code the student sent you. It will create or update their test submission.
results.import_code_placeholder: Paste the student's submission code here…
results.import_error_invalid: Invalid submission code. Make sure you copied the full code from the student.
results.import_error_wrong_test: This submission code is for a different test.

taking.invalid_link_desc: This test link is not valid. Please ask your teacher for a new link.
taking.expired_desc: The deadline was {{date}}. Please contact your teacher.
taking.load_error: This test could not be loaded. Please ask your teacher for a new link.
taking.seb_blocked_title: Open this test in Safe Exam Browser
taking.seb_blocked_desc: This test must be opened in Safe Exam Browser (SEB). Please open the link inside SEB to continue.
taking.seb_blocked_no_bypass: Reloading this page will not bypass this requirement.
taking.live_disclosure: Your teacher can see your work live while you take this test.
taking.draft_restored: Your answers were restored from your last session.
taking.submit_error_db: Submission failed. Your answers are saved below as a backup code.
taking.submitted_desc_db: Your teacher can see your submission in RubricMaker.
taking.submitted_desc_code: Copy the submission code below and send it to your teacher.
taking.drag_to_order: Drag the items below into the correct order.
taking.hot_text_instruction: Click the highlighted fragments to select or deselect them.

monitor.title: Live monitor
monitor.liveDisclosure: Your teacher can see your work live while you take this essay.
monitor.noDatabase: Live monitoring streams presence and proctoring signals over a Supabase Realtime connection, which is not configured for this app. You can still review what happened after the fact: proctoring events are embedded in imported submission codes and stored on each student's test record.
monitor.advisory_note: Presence, drafts, and proctoring flags are advisory signals reported by the student's browser — a determined student can spoof them. Use them to spot patterns, not as definitive proof.

tests.sections_none_hint: No sections yet. Sections help you group questions into labelled parts (e.g. Reading, Listening).
tests.section_empty_hint: No questions in this section yet. Add one below, or drag a question here.
tests.order_items_help: List the items in the correct order. Students will see them in a random order and drag them into the right sequence.
tests.partial_credit_help: Award a fraction of the points for partially correct answers.
tests.cloze_no_gaps: No gaps yet. Click “Insert gap” to add one.
tests.hot_text_passage_placeholder: Type or paste the passage here…
tests.hot_text_fragments_help: Students click highlighted fragments to select or deselect them.

help.multiple_choice_teacher_body: Students pick one option from a list. Mark the correct answer with the checkmark.
help.multiple_response_teacher_body: Students can select more than one correct answer. Mark all correct options.
help.true_false_teacher_body: Students choose between True and False. Set which answer is correct.
help.short_answer_teacher_body: Students type a short text. Optionally add a model answer for auto-scoring.
help.open_teacher_body: Students write a longer response. Scored manually.
help.cloze_teacher_body: Students type missing words. Use {{answer}} in your prompt to mark each gap.
help.cloze_dropdown_teacher_body: Students choose from a dropdown per gap. Use {{correct|wrong1|wrong2}} to define options.
help.matching_teacher_body: Students connect left-side items to the correct right-side items.
help.ordering_teacher_body: Students drag items into the correct sequence. List them in the correct order here.
help.categorize_teacher_body: Students drag items into the correct category. Define categories and items below.
help.hot_text_teacher_body: Students click highlighted text fragments to select the correct ones.
help.multiple_choice_student_body: Read the question and tap the answer you think is correct.
help.multiple_response_student_body: More than one answer can be correct. Tap all answers that apply.
help.true_false_student_body: Decide whether the statement is true or false and tap your answer.
help.short_answer_student_body: Type a short answer in the text field.
help.open_student_body: Write your response in the text area. You can expand it by dragging the corner.
help.cloze_student_body: Type the missing word in each gap marked with a dashed border.
help.cloze_dropdown_student_body: Choose the correct word from each dropdown to complete the sentence.
help.matching_student_body: Connect each item on the left to the correct item on the right.
help.ordering_student_body: Use the arrow buttons to arrange the items in the correct order.
help.categorize_student_body: Drag each item into the correct category.
help.hot_text_student_body: Click or tap the highlighted words that answer the question correctly.

studentsPage.title: Students & Classes
studentsPage.no_students: No students in this class yet.
studentsPage.merge_class_description: Select the target class to move all students into. The current class ({{className}}) will be deleted.
studentsPage.delete_class_confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete {{className}}?
studentsPage.delete_class_warning: The {{count}} student(s) currently in this class will also be permanently deleted. If you want to keep them, merge this class into another one instead.
studentsPage.merge_confirm_description: All students from {{source}} will be moved into {{target}}. The source class will be deleted. This cannot be undone.

gradeStudent.anchor_help_text: Anchor papers are reference submissions used to calibrate your grading. Marking a submission as an anchor lets you compare it side-by-side with new submissions in the grade footer.

rubricBuilder.single_point_notice: Each criterion has one descriptor (the standard). During grading, mark whether the student exceeds, meets, or does not yet meet the standard.
rubricBuilder.regrade_banner_text: This rubric has existing grades. Recalculate scores with the updated point values?
rubricBuilder.unsaved_warning: You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
rubricBuilder.sync_dialog_body: {{count}} submission(s) were graded with this rubric. Their level choices are preserved, but grades will recalculate based on the new point values.
rubricBuilder.insert_speaking_confirm: This will replace all existing criteria with 6 standard speaking dimensions. Continue?
rubricBuilder.insert_speaking_append: Add 6 speaking dimensions to existing criteria?
rubricBuilder.confirm_restore: Restore this version? Current unsaved changes will be lost.
rubricBuilder.single_point_descriptor_label: Proficiency descriptor — what “meets standard” looks like
rubricBuilder.single_point_descriptor_placeholder: Describe what a student does when they meet this standard…
rubricBuilder.level_quality_tip: 💡 Tip: describe what the student does, not just how good it is (e.g. "uses 3+ examples to support each claim")
rubricBuilder.weight_total_warning: Weights total {{total}}% (expected 100%). You can still save — the grade engine normalises them.

peerAnalytics.title: Peer Review Analytics — {{rubricName}}
peerAnalytics.empty_state: No peer reviews have been submitted for this rubric yet.
peerAnalytics.feedback_heatmap_hint: Darker/greener cells indicate criteria that received more written feedback from peer reviewers.

toast.share_code_error: Invalid share code. Please check and try again.
toast.csv_import_error: CSV import failed. Please check the file format.
toast.storage_full: Storage is full — your changes may not have been saved.

toast.invalid_supabase_url_protocol: Invalid protocol: only http:// and https:// are supported.
toast.sync_queued: Offline — changes will sync when you reconnect.

cefr.picker_subtitle: Select statements that this criterion assesses
cefr.student_progress_subtitle: Achieved = avg ≥ {{threshold}}% across graded rubrics with a CEFR target level
cefr.achieve_threshold_help: Score at or above this percentage counts as 'Achieved' on the student profile.

comparativeGrading.title: Comparative: {{name}}
comparativeGrading.select_class_description: Choose which class to grade. Only students from the selected class will appear in the comparison.
comparativeGrading.select_student_description: Pick who to start with in {{class}}, or let the app pair students randomly.
comparativeGrading.no_classes: No classes found. Add a class first.
comparativeGrading.not_enough_students: Not enough students in this class for a comparison. Add at least two students.
comparativeGrading.anchor_changed: Anchor student rotated — starting fresh pairing.
comparativeGrading.unsaved_warning: You have unsaved comparison scores. Leave anyway?
comparativeGrading.session_complete_body: All students have reached the per-student limit of {{count}} comparison(s). All scores have been saved.
comparativeGrading.not_enough_explanation: Comparative grading requires at least two students in the same class. Add another student to this class to get started.

studentPortal.no_linked_account: No student account linked to this email.
studentPortal.no_linked_account_detail: Ask your teacher to add your email address to your student profile so you can log in here.
studentPortal.tour_welcome_content: This is your personal progress page. Here you can see every grade your teacher has given you, along with their feedback and your CEFR language level progress.
studentPortal.tour_stats_content: These cards show the number of assignments you've been graded on, your overall average score, and any self-assessments you've submitted. They update automatically as your teacher grades new work.
studentPortal.tour_content_content: Below this area you'll find a grade history chart showing your score over time, a CEFR progress overview (if your teacher uses language levels), and a full list of each graded assignment with written feedback.
studentPortal.tour_copy_link_content: Use this button to copy a direct link to your personal dashboard. You can share it with a parent or guardian so they can follow your progress too.
studentPortal.self_assess_instruction: Select the level that best matches your own performance for each criterion.
studentPortal.self_assess_reflection_placeholder: What went well? What would you do differently?
studentPortal.essays_load_error: Could not load your essay assignments. Please try refreshing the page.

selfAssess.title: CEFR Self-Assessment
selfAssess.instruction: Read each statement below and tick the ones you feel confident about. Be honest — this helps your teacher understand how you see your own progress.
selfAssess.reflection_help: Optional: write a short note about what you find easy or difficult.
selfAssess.reflection_placeholder: What do you find easy? What are you still working on?
selfAssess.no_descriptors: This rubric has no CEFR descriptors linked yet. Ask your teacher to add them in the rubric builder.
selfAssess.mismatch_overestimate: Student may be overestimating — worth a reflection conversation.
selfAssess.mismatch_underestimate: Student may be underestimating their progress — encourage them!

statistics.select_student_prompt: Select a student to view their statistics.
statistics.student_not_graded: This student hasn't been graded on any rubrics yet.
statistics.radar_min_criteria: At least 3 criteria are needed for the radar chart.

exportPage.no_templates_help_prefix: No templates saved. Upload one in

pronunciationFeedback.word_stress: Watch word stress — try stressing the correct syllable.
pronunciationFeedback.sentence_stress: Focus on stress patterns within sentences.
pronunciationFeedback.connected_speech: Practice linking words in connected speech.
pronunciationFeedback.vowel_sound: Pay attention to vowel quality (e.g., /ɪ/ vs /iː/).
pronunciationFeedback.final_consonant: Don't drop final consonants in words.

speaking.unsaved_warning: You have unsaved scoring. Leave anyway?

speaking.empty_state_no_sessions: No speaking sessions yet. Click "New Session" above to start one.

recordings.local_only_warning: Recordings stay on this device only. Clearing your browser data will delete them.
recordings.video_requires_db: Video recording requires cloud sync to be enabled, since video files are too large to keep only on this device.
recordings.quota_warning: Not enough storage space on this device to start a new recording. Free up space or delete old recordings first.
recordings.too_large_warning: Recording exceeds the 50MB limit and was discarded. Try a shorter recording.
recordings.start_error: Could not start recording. Check your microphone/camera permissions.
recordings.save_error: Failed to save recording — please try again.
recordings.student_disclosure: Audio/video recorded here may be saved with this session and visible to your teacher.

cefrOverview.standards_empty: No standards linked to rubrics for this student.
cefrOverview.empty_no_cefr: No CEFR data yet. Grade rubrics with a CEFR target level or have the student complete a self-assessment.
cefrOverview.select_student_prompt: Select a class and student to view their CEFR overview.
cefrOverview.class_empty: No students found. Add students and link rubrics with CEFR target levels.

notifications.all_up_to_date: All students graded within the last {{threshold}} days.
notifications.permission_denied: Browser notifications blocked. Allow them in browser settings.

onboarding.title: Welcome to RubricMaker
onboarding.subtitle: Let's set up your account in a few quick steps.
onboarding.role_teacher_desc: Create rubrics, grade students, and view statistics.
onboarding.role_admin_desc: Manage users, schools, and retention settings.
onboarding.school_id_placeholder: Paste the school ID shared by your admin
onboarding.role_student_desc: View your grades and complete essay assignments.
onboarding.student_linked_body: Your teacher has linked your account to {{name}} at {{school}}.
onboarding.done_body: Your school is set up. You can now start creating rubrics and grading students.
onboarding.student_done_body: Your account is set up as a student. You'll be redirected to your portal once your teacher links your school email to a student profile.
onboarding.error_role_update: Could not update your role. Please try again.

admin.title: Admin Dashboard
admin.archive_desc: Archived students are removed from class rosters but their grades are preserved. Anonymize to erase personal data.
admin.btn_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this school? This cannot be undone.
admin.anonymize_confirm: Replace this student's name, email and student number with an anonymous token? This cannot be undone.
admin.delete_student_confirm: Permanently delete this student and all their grades? This cannot be undone.
admin.retention_desc: Students whose most recent grade is older than the retention period will be automatically anonymized.
admin.retention_per_school: Set retention period per school on the Schools tab.
admin.anon_auth_desc: Students using Safe Exam Browser cannot sign in with OTP. Anonymous sign-ins let them access essay assignments without a password or email code.
admin.anon_auth_hint: Enable 'Allow anonymous sign-ins' in your Supabase project under Authentication → Configuration, then redeploy.
admin.users_offline_title: User management requires a database connection
admin.users_offline_body: Connect a Supabase database to manage user roles and view all registered accounts. See the Deployment documentation to configure cloud sync.
admin.schools_offline_title: School management requires a database connection
admin.schools_offline_body: Connect a Supabase database to create and manage schools, set data retention policies, and view school members.

essay_assignment.deadline_help: optional — students cannot submit after this date/time
essay_assignment.db_embed_help: Students will verify their email via OTP, then submit directly to your Supabase project. No copy-paste code needed. The backup code is still shown as a receipt.
essay_assignment.save_template_title: Save this essay configuration for later use — pre-fills the form next time you open this rubric

essays.create_first_instruction: Create your first essay assignment to get started.
essays.delete_essay_warning: This will permanently delete "{{name}}" and all of its assignments and submissions.
essays.no_new_students: All selected students are already assigned to this essay.
essays.import_error: This code could not be read. Please check it and try again.
essays.import_wrong_essay: This submission code belongs to a different essay.

essay.email_gate_desc: Your email is used to link your essay to your account. No password or code needed.
essay.invalid_link_desc: This essay link is not valid. Please ask your teacher for a new link.
essay.expired_desc: The deadline was {{date}}. Please contact your teacher.
essay.seb_blocked: This exam must be opened in Safe Exam Browser. Submission is blocked until you reopen this link inside SEB.
essay.draft_restored: Draft restored from your last session.
essay.submitted_desc_db: Your teacher can see your submission in RubricMaker. Keep the backup code below just in case.
essay.submitted_desc_code: Copy the submission code below and send it to your teacher.
essay.time_up_auto: Time is up — your essay was submitted automatically.
essay.time_up_over_submitted: Time is up — your essay was submitted with {{count}} extra words.
essay.expired_desc_no_date: This assignment has expired. Please contact your teacher.
essay.email_invalid: Enter a valid school email address.
essay.session_error: Could not start session. Ask your teacher to enable anonymous sign-ins in Supabase.

studentProfile.portfolio_empty: No activity yet for this student.
studentProfile.no_grades_yet: No graded rubrics yet for this student.

cambridge.show_labels_help: Display the matching Cambridge English Qualification (e.g. "B2 First") next to CEFR level badges.
cambridge.lookup_failed: Couldn't find Cambridge data for this word.
cambridge.lookup_success: Word details updated from Cambridge Dictionary.
cambridge.docs_body: When enabled in Settings, CEFR level badges show the matching Cambridge English Qualification (e.g. A2 Key, B1 Preliminary, B2 First, C1 Advanced, C2 Proficiency) as a subtle secondary label. In the Rubric Builder's vocabulary panel, if a Cambridge Dictionary API key is configured in Admin → Integrations, each word gets a "Look up" button that fetches its CEFR level and definition automatically.

vocabProfile.page_subtitle: CEFR vocabulary distribution per class and student, based on analysed documents.
vocabProfile.chart_aria_label: Stacked bar chart of CEFR vocabulary level distribution
vocabProfile.empty_chart: No vocabulary data yet. Analyse student documents to see CEFR vocabulary distributions here.

csv.sync_label: Sync class rosters (remove students not in this CSV)
